long_reaction_chains.name: Long Reaction Chains
long_reaction_chains.description: Multiple reaction steps where each step's product becomes the reactant for the next

structural_patterns: Product of one step becomes reactant of next

counting_enumeration.name: Counting/Enumeration
counting_enumeration.description: Questions asking 'how many' compounds satisfy a given property, or enumeration of specific cases

deep_mechanistic_reasoning.name: Deep Mechanistic Reasoning
deep_mechanistic_reasoning.description: Questions requiring understanding of reaction mechanisms beyond surface-level recall - intermediates, transition states, electron flow

comparative_ranking.name: Comparative Ranking
comparative_ranking.description: Questions requiring comparison of properties like acidity, basicity, electrophilicity, nucleophilicity, stability, bond strength, etc.

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements regarding the energy of the stationary state is true in the following one-electron system? (1) -1.09 × 10⁻¹⁸ J for second orbit of H atom (2) +2.18 × 10⁻¹⁸ J for second orbit of He⁺ ion (3) +8.72 × 10⁻¹⁸ J for first orbit of He⁺ ion (4) -2.18 × 10⁻¹⁸ J for third orbit of Li²⁺ ion
questions.sub_topic: Bohr model - energy of hydrogen-like species
questions.solution_summary: Eₙ = -2.18 × 10⁻¹⁸ × Z²/n² J/atom. For 3rd orbit of Li²⁺: Z=3, n=3 → E = -2.18 × 10⁻¹⁸ × 9/9 = -2.18 × 10⁻¹⁸ J. Only option 4 is correct.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Bohr's energy formula for hydrogen-like species.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following graphs accurately represents the plot of partial pressure of CS₂ vs its mole fraction in a mixture of acetone and CS₂ at constant temperature?
questions.solution_summary: Acetone-CS₂ mixture shows positive deviation from Raoult's law because the intermolecular forces between acetone and CS₂ are weaker than those between like molecules. The partial pressure of CS₂ is higher than predicted by Raoult's law.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of positive deviation from Raoult's law for acetone-CS₂ mixture.

questions.question_text: The correct trend in the first ionization enthalpies of the elements in the 3rd period of periodic table is: (1) Al < Si < S < P < Cl (2) Al < S < P < Si < Cl (3) Si < S < Al < P < Cl (4) S < Si < Al < P < Cl
questions.solution_summary: Ionisation enthalpy generally increases across a period: Al < Si < S < P < Cl. P has higher IE than S due to half-filled p³ configuration (extra stability).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of ionisation enthalpy trends with exception for phosphorus.

questions.question_text: The correct sequence of reagents for the conversion of X to Y is:
questions.sub_topic: Reagent sequence for functional group transformation
questions.solution_summary: The correct sequence: (i) NaOEt (for deprotonation/alkylation), (ii) B₂H₆/H₂O₂ (hydroboration-oxidation), (iii) Jones reagent (oxidation of alcohol to carbonyl).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of multi-step synthesis and reagent selection.

questions.question_text: In the given electrochemical cell, Ag(s)|AgCl(s)|FeCl₂(aq),FeCl₃(aq)|Pt(s) at 298 K, the cell potential (E_cell) will increase when: (A) Concentration of Fe²⁺ is increased. (B) Concentration of Fe³⁺ is decreased. (C) Concentration of Fe²⁺ is decreased. (D) Concentration of Fe³⁺ is increased. (E) Concentration of Cl⁻ is increased.
questions.sub_topic: Nernst equation - effect of concentration on E_cell
questions.solution_summary: Cell reaction: Cl⁻ + Ag + Fe³⁺ → Fe²⁺ + AgCl. E_cell = E°_cell - (0.059/1)log([Fe²⁺]/([Cl⁻][Fe³⁺])). To increase E_cell: decrease [Fe²⁺] (C) or increase [Fe³⁺] (D) or increase [Cl⁻] (E). So C, D and E only.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Nernst equation to determine concentration effects on cell potential.

questions.question_text: 'x' is the product which is obtained from propanenitrile and stannous chloride in the presence of hydrochloric acid followed by hydrolysis. 'y' is the product which is obtained from but-2-ene by ozonolysis followed by hydrolysis. From the following, which product is not obtained when one mole of 'x' and one mole of 'y' react with each other in the presence of alkali followed by heating?
questions.sub_topic: Stephen reduction, ozonolysis and aldol condensation
questions.solution_summary: x = propanal (from Stephen reduction of propanenitrile). y = acetaldehyde (from ozonolysis of but-2-ene). Aldol condensation of propanal and acetaldehyde gives various products. 3-Methylbut-2-enal is not obtained.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Multi-step reaction sequence involving named reactions and product identification.

questions.question_text: Consider the following sequence of reactions. Assuming that the reaction proceeds to completion, then 137 mg of 4-nitrotoluene will produce ____ mg of B. (Given molar mass in g mol⁻¹ H:1, C:12, N:14, O:16, Br:80)
questions.solution_summary: Moles of 4-nitrotoluene = 137 × 10⁻³ / 137 = 0.001 mol. Product B has molar mass 228 g/mol. Mass of B = 0.001 × 228 = 0.228 g = 228 mg.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Stoichiometric calculation from multi-step reaction sequence.

questions.question_text: A cup of water at 5°C (system) is placed in a microwave oven and the oven is turned on for one minute during which, the water begins to boil. Which of the following option is true?
questions.solution_summary: Heat is given to the system (q = +ve). Work done is negative (w = -ve) as liquid expands to gas. Internal energy increases (ΔU = +ve) as temperature increases from 5°C to 100°C.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires understanding of sign conventions for heat, work and internal energy.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: Statement I: [CoBr₄]²⁻ ion will absorb light of lower energy than [CoCl₄]²⁻ ion. Statement II: In [CoI₄]²⁻ ion, the energy separation between the two set of d-orbitals is more than [CoCl₄]²⁻ ion.
questions.solution_summary: Statement I is true: Cl⁻ is stronger ligand than Br⁻, so Δt for [CoCl₄]²⁻ > [CoBr₄]²⁻, thus [CoBr₄]²⁻ absorbs lower energy light. Statement II is false: I⁻ is weaker ligand than Cl⁻, so Δt for [CoI₄]²⁻ < [CoCl₄]²⁻.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of ligand field strengths from spectrochemical series.

questions.question_text: But-2-yne and hydrogen (one mole each) are separately treated with (i) Pd/C and (ii) Na/liq. NH₃ to give the products X and Y respectively. Identify the incorrect statements. A. X and Y are stereoisomers. B. Dipole moment of X is zero. C. Boiling point of X is higher than Y. D. X and Y react with O₃/Zn + H₂O to give different products.
questions.solution_summary: X = cis-2-butene (from Pd/C hydrogenation), Y = trans-2-butene (from Na/liq.NH₃ reduction). A: X and Y are stereoisomers (true). B: Dipole moment of X is NOT zero (cis-isomer has dipole), so false. C: Boiling point of X (cis) is higher than Y (trans) (true). D: Both give same product (2-butanone) on ozonolysis, so false.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of cis-trans isomers and their properties.

questions.question_text: Given, (A) n = 5, m_l = -1; (B) n = 3, l = 2, m_l = -1, m_s = +1/2. The maximum number of electron(s) in an atom that can have the quantum numbers as given in (A) and (B) respectively are:
questions.solution_summary: (A) n=5, m_l=-1: For n=5, l can be 1,2,3,4 each having m_l=-1, each subshell can hold 2 electrons. Total = 4×2 = 8 electrons. (B) n=3, l=2, m_l=-1, m_s=+1/2: Only 1 electron possible.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires counting maximum electrons for given quantum number sets.

questions.question_text: Consider the following compounds: Arrange these compounds in the increasing order of reactivity with nitrating mixture.
questions.solution_summary: Reactivity towards nitration depends on electron donating/withdrawing groups. OMe (activating, o/p-directing) > Cl (deactivating, o/p-directing) > NO₂ (strongly deactivating, m-directing). Order: b < a < c.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of substituent effects on electrophilic substitution.

questions.question_text: The statements that are incorrect about the nickel (II) complex of dimethylglyoxime are: A. It is red in colour. B. It has a high solubility in water at pH = 9. C. The Ni ion has two unpaired d-electrons. D. The N-Ni-N bond angle is almost close to 90°. E. The complex contains four five-membered metallacycles (metal containing rings).
questions.solution_summary: A: It is rosy red ppt (true). B: It is precipitated in basic medium, not highly soluble (false). C: Ni²⁺ has d⁸ configuration in square planar dsp² complex, 0 unpaired electrons (false). D: N-Ni-N bond angle is ~90° (true). E: Only 2 five-membered rings are formed, not 4 (false).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires knowledge of Ni-DMG complex structure and properties.

questions.question_text: From the given following (A to D) cyclic structures, those which will not react with Tollen's reagent are:
questions.solution_summary: Tollen's reagent oxidises aldehydes (including aldoses). Structures B and D do not have free aldehyde group (are ketoses or have no free anomeric OH) and will not react with Tollen's reagent.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires identification of structures that give positive Tollen's test.

questions.question_text: Identify the molecule (X) with maximum number of lone pairs of electrons (obtained using Lewis dot structure) among HNO₃, H₂SO₄, NF₃ and O₃. Choose the correct bond angle made by the central atom of the molecule (X).
questions.solution_summary: Number of lone pairs: HNO₃ (6), H₂SO₄ (8), NF₃ (10 - maximum), O₃ (6). X = NF₃. Bond angle in NF₃ is approximately 102° (due to lone pair repulsion, less than tetrahedral 109.5°).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires counting lone pairs and determining bond angle from VSEPR theory.

questions.question_text: Match List-I with List-II: List-I (Functional group detection): A. Unsaturation (Baeyer's test); B. Alcoholic group (Ceric ammonium nitrate test); C. Aldehyde group (Tollen's reagent); D. Phenolic group (FeCl₃ test). List-II (Change observed during detection): I. Red colour appears; II. Silver mirror appears; III. Violet colour appears; IV. Discharge of pink colour.
questions.solution_summary: Baeyer's test (Unsaturation) → Discharge of pink colour (A-IV); Ceric ammonium nitrate (Alcohol) → Red colour appears (B-I); Tollen's reagent (Aldehyde) → Silver mirror appears (C-II); FeCl₃ (Phenol) → Violet colour appears (D-III).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Matching of functional group tests with observations - pure recall.

questions.question_text: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: Sublimation is used for the separation and purification of compounds with low melting point. Statement-II: The boiling point of a liquid increases as the external pressure is reduced.
questions.solution_summary: Statement I is false: Sublimation is used for compounds with high vapour pressure (not low melting point). Statement II is false: Boiling point decreases (not increases) when external pressure is reduced.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Factual recall of sublimation and boiling point relationship with pressure.

questions.question_text: Compound 'P' undergoes the following sequence of reactions: Identify 'P'.
questions.solution_summary: Based on the reaction sequence and product analysis, compound P is option 2.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires tracking of reaction sequence to identify starting compound.

questions.question_text: The correct statements from the following are: (A) Ionic radii of trivalent cations of group 13 elements decreases down the group. (B) Electronegativity of group 13 elements decreases down the group. (C) Among the group 13 elements, Boron has highest first ionisation enthalpy. (D) The trichloride and triiodide of group 13 elements are covalent in nature.
questions.solution_summary: (A) False: Ionic radii increase down the group (B³⁺ < Al³⁺ < Ga³⁺ < In³⁺ < Tl³⁺). (B) False: Electronegativity order is B > Tl > In > Ga > Al. (C) True: Boron has highest IE. (D) True: Trichlorides and triiodides are covalent.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires comparison of periodic trends in group 13 elements.

questions.question_text: Consider the general reaction given below at 400K: xA(g) ⇌ yB(g). The values of Kp and Kc are studied under the same condition of temperature but variation in x and y. (i) Kp = 85.87 and Kc = 2.586; (ii) Kp = 0.862 and Kc = 28.62. The value of x and y in (i) and (ii) respectively are:

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of Kp-Kc relationship to determine stoichiometric coefficients.

questions.question_text: For the thermal decomposition of reaction AB(g), the following is constructed. The half life of the reaction is 'x' min. x = ____ min. (Nearest integer)
questions.sub_topic: Half-life from concentration vs time data
questions.solution_summary: From graph: [AB]₀ = 0.60 M, [AB] at 100 min = 0.55 M. For zero order: [AB]₀ - [AB]t = kt. k = (0.60 - 0.55)/100 = 5×10⁻⁴ M/min. t₁/₂ = [AB]₀/(2k) = 0.60/(2×5×10⁻⁴) = 600 min = 10 min.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Application of zero order kinetics to determine half-life from graph data.

questions.question_text: For the following gas phase equilibrium reaction at constant temperature, NH₃(g) ⇌ ½N₂(g) + ³⁄₂H₂(g). If the total pressure is √3 atm and the pressure equilibrium constant (Kp) is 9 atm, then the degree of dissociation is given as (x × 10⁻²)^(-1/2). The value of x is (Nearest integer).
questions.solution_summary: NH₃ → ½N₂ + ³⁄₂H₂. Let initial moles = 1, α = degree of dissociation. Total moles at equilibrium = 1 + α. Partial pressures: P_NH₃ = P(1-α)/(1+α), P_N₂ = P(α/2)/(1+α), P_H₂ = P(3α/2)/(1+α). Kp = (P_N₂)^(1/2)(P_H₂)^(3/2)/(P_NH₃) = P·α²/(1-α²). Given P = √3, Kp = 9. α²/(1-α²) = 9/√3 = 3√3. α² = (3√3)/(1+3√3) ≈ 0.008. α ≈ 0.089. α = (125 × 10⁻²)^(-1/2) → x = 125.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Complex equilibrium calculation involving degree of dissociation and Kp.

questions.question_text: x mg of pure HCl was used to make an aqueous solution. 25.0 mL of 0.1 M Ba(OH)₂ solution is used when the HCl solution was titrated against it. The numerical value of x is ____ × 10⁻¹. (Nearest integer). Given: Molar mass of HCl and Ba(OH)₂ are 36.5 and 171.0 g mol⁻¹ respectively.
questions.solution_summary: Ba(OH)₂ + 2HCl → BaCl₂ + 2H₂O. mmol Ba(OH)₂ = 25 × 0.1 = 2.5 mmol. mmol HCl = 2 × 2.5 = 5 mmol. Mass HCl = 5 × 36.5 = 182.5 mg. x = 1825 × 10⁻¹.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Stoichiometric calculation from acid-base titration.

questions.question_text: Consider all the structural isomers with molecular formula C₅H₁₁Br are separately treated with KOH (aq) to give respective substitution products, without any rearrangement. The number of products which can exhibit optical isomerism from these is ____.
questions.solution_summary: C₅H₁₁Br has 8 structural isomers. On hydrolysis with aq. KOH, alcohols are formed. Among the products, those with chiral centres exhibit optical isomerism. The products that can show optical activity are 3 (considering one enantiomer).

archetype_analysis.reasoning: Requires identification of chiral products from substitution of C₅H₁₁Br isomers.

questions.question_text: The crystal field splitting energy of [Co(oxalate)₃]³⁻ complex is 'n' times that of the [Cr(oxalate)₃]³⁻ complex. Here 'n' is ____. [Assume Δ₀ ≫ P]
questions.solution_summary: [Co(ox)₃]³⁻: Co³⁺ (d⁶) in strong field → t₂g⁶ e_g⁰. CFSE = 6 × (-0.4Δ₀) = -2.4Δ₀. [Cr(ox)₃]³⁻: Cr³⁺ (d³) → t₂g³. CFSE = 3 × (-0.4Δ₀) = -1.2Δ₀. Ratio = 2.4/1.2 = 2.

archetype_analysis.reasoning: CFSE calculation for d⁶ and d³ configurations in octahedral field.
